prevalence.source: Urology epidemiology syntheses; regional climate risk context
prevalence.trend: increasing with heat exposure, obesity, and dietary patterns

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; high acute pain and recurrence burden

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical renal colic with urinalysis; imaging with ultrasound or CT; metabolic evaluation for recurrent stones

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; recurrence prevention depends on counseling and follow-up

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Ultrasound commonly used in SUS; CT for complex cases in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Hydration, analgesia, alpha-blockers for stone passage; ESWL/ureteroscopy/PCNL for larger stones; prevention counseling
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited lithotripsy access outside major cities and long wait times for elective procedures
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Effective acute management pathways and growing preventive counseling in urology services
